genre: space exploration with real NASA/ISRO facts

story.genre: space exploration with real NASA/ISRO facts

parts.title: Stargazer
parts.text: Every night, Leo would make a polite **request** to stay up past her bedtime so she could stargaze from 8 to 9 PM. She was no longer **ignorant** about the night sky — she had studied it for two years. Her old telescope had a **hollow** tube, light as a feather, that she had repaired herself with tape and patience.

parts.title: Mission Control
parts.text: One evening a **torrent** of alerts filled the science news sites — a massive solar storm had disrupted satellite signals around the world. After a **drought** of cloudy nights that had kept her from the telescope for two whole weeks, Leo finally had clear skies again. She began to **chatter** excitedly, explaining to Zara and Tara everything she knew about solar **cycle**s and how they repeat over roughly eleven years. A travelling science **merchant** had once sold Leo a used data-logging device, and now she used it to record every spike and dip in the readings.

parts.title: The Discovery
parts.text: While analyzing the data, Leo noticed something unusual about the **texture** of the wave pattern — it was rougher and more layered than anything described in her books. The data seemed to **imply** that a new type of solar wave was occurring, one that scientists had not yet named. She hoped the signal would not **vanish** before she could save it, so she **recover**ed a backup copy from her cloud drive just in case.

parts.title: A Star Is Born
parts.text: Leo submitted her findings to ISRO. Three weeks later, she received a letter — her discovery was real. A new solar wave pattern was named after her. She was invited to visit ISRO's Space Applications Centre in Ahmedabad. "You have the mind of a true scientist," the director told her. Leo knew that this was just the beginning of her journey to the stars.

definitions.meaning1: The act of politely asking for something. Example: She made a request to the librarian for a book about dinosaurs.
definitions.meaning2: To ask for something politely. Example: I would like to request a seat by the window, please.
definitions.studentChallenge: The student wrote a ___ letter asking the head teacher for permission to start a gardening club.

definitions.meaning1: A long period of time with very little rain. Example: The drought caused the crops to die.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ lasted for three months and the rivers dried up.

definitions.meaning1: Having an empty space inside. Example: The squirrel stored acorns in the hollow tree trunk.
definitions.meaning2: A small valley or dip in the land. Example: The deer gathered in the grassy hollow between the two hills.
definitions.studentChallenge: When I knocked on the old log, it made a ___ sound because it was empty inside.

definitions.meaning1: To suggest something without saying it directly. Example: Her smile implied that she was happy.
definitions.studentChallenge: His silence seemed to ___ that he agreed.

definitions.meaning1: A series of events that repeat in the same order. Example: The water cycle includes evaporation and rain.
definitions.meaning2: To ride a bicycle. Example: She cycles to school every day.
definitions.studentChallenge: Spring, summer, fall, winter — the ___ of seasons repeats.

definitions.meaning1: Not knowing about something or lacking knowledge. Example: Before studying the ocean, I was ignorant about how deep it really is.
definitions.studentChallenge: He was ___ of the rules because he had missed the first day of class.

definitions.meaning1: To get better after being ill or hurt. Example: It took two weeks for him to fully recover from the flu and return to school.
definitions.meaning2: To get something back that was lost. Example: The divers managed to recover the treasure chest from the bottom of the sea.
definitions.studentChallenge: The doctor said she would ___ quickly if she rested and drank plenty of fluids.

definitions.meaning1: A person who buys and sells goods for a living. Example: The merchant in the old town square sold beautiful handmade pottery.
definitions.studentChallenge: The silk ___ traveled from town to town selling his colorful fabrics.

definitions.meaning1: A strong, fast-moving stream of water or a heavy downpour of rain. Example: A torrent of rainwater rushed down the hillside and filled the creek in minutes.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ of water from the broken pipe flooded the basement in no time.

definitions.meaning1: The way something feels when you touch it or how it looks like it would feel. Example: The texture of the tree bark was rough and bumpy under her fingertips.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ of the silk ribbon was smooth and soft compared to the scratchy wool.

definitions.meaning1: To talk quickly about things that are not very important. Example: The excited students began to chatter about their weekend plans as soon as the bell rang.
definitions.meaning2: To make quick, short sounds. Example: The squirrel seemed to chatter angrily at us from the tree branch.
definitions.studentChallenge: The monkeys would ___ loudly whenever visitors walked past their enclosure.

definitions.meaning1: To disappear suddenly and completely. Example: The magician waved his wand, and the rabbit seemed to vanish into thin air.
definitions.studentChallenge: The fog began to ___ as the morning sun rose higher in the sky.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Leo made a polite _(1)_ to her science teacher, asking for extra time in the lab after school. She wanted to study the _(2)_ — the repeating pattern of a certain star's brightness, which grew and faded over and over. She was no longer _(3)_ about stars — she had read every book she could find and knew the subject well. The old telescope she used had a _(4)_ tube, empty inside and light as a feather. Its metal surface had a rough _(5)_ — bumpy and uneven under her fingertips from years of use. After a long _(6)_ without clear skies, cloudy and dry for weeks, the stars finally appeared. A _(7)_ of light — a fast, powerful beam — shot across her lens from a passing satellite. She began to _(8)_ excitedly, talking quickly and barely stopping for breath as she described what she saw. The sight seemed to _(9)_ that something unusual was happening far out in space. She hoped the data would not _(10)_ before she could save it, disappearing from the screen without warning. A traveling _(11)_ of astronomy books had visited the school fair, and Leo had bought one that showed her how to _(12)_ data lost from a corrupted file.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
